description_long: This is a compact, cast-iron radiator with a classic design, featuring four vertical columns connected by rounded top and bottom headers. The radiator is painted in a muted gray color, with a slightly glossy finish that highlights its robust, industrial character. Each column has elongated vertical slots, allowing for efficient heat dissipation and a distinctive visual rhythm. At the top and bottom of the front face, there are two prominent hexagonal caps, likely for venting and water inlet/outlet, with visible manual valve handles on the sides. The base of the radiator is supported by small, sturdy feet, elevating it slightly above the ground. The overall construction suggests durability and functionality, suitable for use in urban interiors such as apartments, garages, or utility rooms.
description: A compact, gray cast-iron radiator with four vertical columns, hexagonal caps, and manual valve handles, finished in a slightly glossy paint.
description_view_0: Front view showing the four vertical columns, central hexagonal caps at top and bottom, and a valve handle at the base.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the radiator, the spacing between columns, and two manual valve handles on either side.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with visible hexagonal caps and a valve handle at the bottom.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the depth, column spacing, and both manual valve handles.
attribute_pipe_connections: Two pipe connections, one at the bottom center front and one at the bottom center rear, both oriented horizontally outward.
